perturbation.original_text: 1. “Confidential Information” means and includes all data, documents and information disclosed or to be disclosed by either Party to other Party and its Representatives (as defined herein) (including i nformation transmitted in written, electronic, magnetic or other form and also information transmitted orally or visually) and any and all information which may be developed or created, in whole or in part, directly or indirectly, from such information including all notes, summaries, an alyses, compilations and other writings.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Confidential Information” means and includes all data, documents and information disclosed or to be disclosed by either Party to other Party and its Representatives (including i nformation transmitted in written, electronic, magnetic or other form and also information transmitted orally or visually).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'and any and all information which may be developed or created, in whole or in part, directly or indirectly, from such information including all notes, summaries, an alyses, compilations and other writings', the definition of Confidential Information becomes narrower. This creates a contradiction because later clauses discussing the use and protection of Confidential Information may implicitly assume the original, broader definition, especially clause 3, which speaks of 'information which may be developed or created'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information is valuable, proprietary and confidential. Unless other wise expressly authorized by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence for a period as stipulated in Clause 7 herein, and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or any employee or agent (except its officers, employees, representatives, advisors or agents who have a need to know) and shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the purposes agree to herein. No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information' broadly to include information derived from disclosed information (notes, summaries, etc.). The changed text omits this part of the definition, narrowing its scope. Clause 3 (Contradicted Text) speaks of protecting 'Confidential Information' without explicitly excluding the derived information that is now omitted from the definition. Because the changed text omits a core element of the original definition, the Receiving party now has to only protect the original information, whereas the contradicted text may be interpreted to include derived information. This creates a strong inconsistency regarding what information must be protected and used only for the purposes agreed, leading to a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Receiving Party agrees, except to the extent pursuant to, or required by law, regulation, legal process or regula tory authority to disclose any Confidential Information (prior intimation be provided to Disclosing Party to enable it to take recourse of legal remedies viz, injunctions, stay etc.), Receiving Party shall not (i) use any portion of the Confidential Inform ation for any purpose not contemplated by this Agreement; or (ii) disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Receiving Party agrees, Receiving Party shall not (i) use any portion of the Confidential Inform ation for any purpose not contemplated by this Agreement; or (ii) disclose the Confidential Information or any part of it to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the phrase 'except to the extent pursuant to, or required by law, regulation, legal process or regula tory authority to disclose any Confidential Information (prior intimation be provided to Disclosing Party to enable it to take recourse of legal remedies viz, injunctions, stay etc.)', the clause becomes overly restrictive. This omission creates an internal contradiction because in reality, legal processes may require disclosure. This strict prohibition contradicts potential legal obligations and makes the agreement unenforceable under certain circumstances, particularly if a court orders disclosure. Also it contradicts Section 2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, each party ackn owledges that confidential information shall not include any information which:  Is or becomes known to public through no wrongful act; or  Is already known to receiving Party from the third party without breach of this agreement; or  Is rightfully received by Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; or  Is independently developed by Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; or  Is required to be disclosed b Receiving Party in compliance of orders of competent Court or, any appropriately empow ered Government Agency or under any statute.  Is explicitely approved / permitted by disclosure by written authority of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for disclosure of confidential information if required by law, regulation, legal process, or regulatory authority, with prior intimation to the disclosing party. The changed text removes this exception, creating a blanket prohibition on disclosure. This contradicts Section 2, which explicitly states that confidential information does not include information required to be disclosed by court order or government agency. Thus, the changed text directly conflicts with Section 2, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Notwithstanding the disclosure of any Confidential Informat ion by the Disclosing Party to Rece iving Party, the Disclosing Party shall retain title and all intellectual property and proprietary rights in the Confidential Information. No license under any trade mark, patents or copy right, or application for same which are now or thereafter may be ob tained by Disclosing Party is either granted or implied by the conveying of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party shall not conceal, alter, obliterate, mutilate, deface or otherwise interfere with any trade mark, trade mark notice, copy right notic e, confidentiality notice or any notice of any other proprietary right of the Disclosing Party on any copy of the Confidential Information, and shall reproduce any such mark or notice on all copies of such Confidential Information. Likewise, the Receiving Party shall not add or emboss its own or any other mark, symbol or logo on such Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Notwithstanding the disclosure of any Confidential Informat ion by the Disclosing Party to Rece iving Party, the Disclosing Party shall retain title and all intellectual property and proprietary rights in the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall not conceal, alter, obliterate, mutilate, deface or otherwise interfere with any trade mark, trade mark notice, copy right notic e, confidentiality notice or any notice of any other proprietary right of the Disclosing Party on any copy of the Confidential Information, and shall reproduce any such mark or notice on all copies of such Confidential Information. Likewise, the Receiving Party shall not add or emboss its own or any other mark, symbol or logo on such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence 'No license under any trade mark, patents or copy right, or application for same which are now or thereafter may be ob tained by Disclosing Party is either granted or implied by the conveying of Confidential Information.', it creates uncertainty about the permitted use of the disclosed information. This contradicts the overall purpose of the NDA, which is to allow for evaluation and potential business dealings. While title remains with the disclosing party, the lack of clarity about implied licenses opens the door to legal disputes, particularly when evaluating the information for potential business use. It contradicts clause 3 which has the phrase 'No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information is valuable, proprietary and confidential. Unless other wise expressly authorized by Disclosing Party, Receiving Party agrees to retain the Confidential Information in confidence for a period as stipulated in Clause 7 herein, and shall not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party or any employee or agent (except its officers, employees, representatives, advisors or agents who have a need to know) and shall not use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than for the purposes agree to herein. No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly denies any implied licenses, which aligns with the contradicted text in clause 3 that states 'No license expressed or implied in the Confi dential Information is granted to either party other than to use the information in the minor and to the extent authorised by this Agreement.' By removing the sentence denying implied licenses, the changed text introduces uncertainty about permitted uses, especially concerning evaluation and potential business dealings. This directly contradicts the clause 3, which limits the use of confidential information and specifies that no implied license is granted, causing a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. This Agreement shall be effective from the date hereof and shall terminate upon the completion of any transaction in relation to the underlying transacti on or otherwise as determined by the parties; however, the obligations of Receiving Party respecting disclosure and confidentiality shall continue to be binding and applicable without limit in time except and, until such information enters the public domai n save and except the clause (2) herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. This Agreement shall be effective from the date hereof and shall terminate upon the completion of any transaction in relation to the underlying transacti on or otherwise as determined by the parties; however, the obligations of Receiving Party respecting disclosure and confidentiality shall continue to be binding and applicable without limit in time.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'except and, until such information enters the public domai n save and except the clause (2) herein.', the clause now suggests confidentiality obligations are perpetual, regardless of whether the information becomes publicly available or exceptions mentioned in clause 2. This contradicts the exceptions explicitly stated in Clause 2, which outlines situations where information is *not* considered confidential (e.g., becomes public knowledge through no fault of the receiving party, or required by law). The omission makes the confidentiality obligations unrealistically broad and potentially unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Notwithstanding any other provisions of this Agreement, each party ackn owledges that confidential information shall not include any information which:  Is or becomes known to public through no wrongful act; or  Is already known to receiving Party from the third party without breach of this agreement; or  Is rightfully received by Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; or  Is independently developed by Receiving Party without breach of this Agreement; or  Is required to be disclosed b Receiving Party in compliance of orders of competent Court or, any appropriately empow ered Government Agency or under any statute.  Is explicitely approved / permitted by disclosure by written authority of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for confidentiality obligations to cease when information enters the public domain or based on clause 2 exceptions. The changed text removes these exceptions, stating the obligations are 'without limit in time'. This creates a direct contradiction with Clause 2, which explicitly defines situations where information is not considered confidential (e.g., becomes publicly known without fault). Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, making enforcement unclear and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 15. No person of the Bank or the Contractors and third parties shall violate the Social media Policy of the Bank. 16. The following acts on the part of personnel of the Bank or Contractors and third parties shall be construed as violation of Social Media Policy : a. Non-adherence to the standards / guidelines in relation to So cial Media Policy issued by the Bank from time to time. b. Any omission or commission which exposes the Bank to actual or potential monetary loss or otherwise, reputation loss on account of non - adherence of Social Media related systems and procedures.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. No person of the Bank or the Contractors and third parties shall adhere to guidelines relating to Social media usage. 16. Personnel should be mindful to avoid actions which exposes the Bank to actual or potential monetary loss or otherwise, reputation loss.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the explicit reference to the 'Social Media Policy of the Bank' with the vague 'guidelines relating to Social media usage' and omitting specific examples of violations, the agreement loses clarity and enforceability. This creates contradiction because the original clause 15 states that social media policy of the bank should not be violated, and the changed text does not reffered to that. It also contradicts intent behind clause 15.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. No person of the Bank or the Contractors and third parties shall adhere to guidelines relating to Social media usage.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies adherence to the 'Social Media Policy of the Bank' and lists examples of violations. The changed text replaces this with a vague reference to 'guidelines relating to Social media usage' and omits the specific examples. Because Clause 15 is about the Social Media Policy and now it is about 'guidelines relating to Social media usage', it directly contradicts Clause 15 which is the contradicted text.
